examples.sentence: Please, have a heart and reconsider firing John; he's been going through a tough time lately.

examples.sentence: If you don't have a heart, how can you expect to understand what others are feeling?

examples.sentence: You have a heart, so you can feel love and empathy towards others.

examples.sentence: Even robots with artificial intelligence don't have a heart like humans do.

examples.sentence: After the party, Jake definitely had a jag on and was stumbling around the room.

examples.sentence: Do you think Sarah has a jag on tonight? She seems a bit unsteady on her feet.

examples.sentence: I had a laugh watching that funny movie last night; it really lifted my spirits.
examples.translation: I laughed watching that funny movie last night; it really lifted my spirits.

examples.sentence: Did you have a laugh at the party, or was it more serious than usual?
examples.translation: Did you laugh at the party, or was it more serious than usual?

examples.translation: Let's laugh together to ease the stress after a long day of work.

examples.sentence: Tom always has a laugh at his brother’s expense, teasing him about his clumsiness.
examples.translation: Tom always jokes at his brother’s expense, teasing him about his clumsiness.

examples.sentence: They had a laugh poking fun at their teacher’s quirky habits, but it was all in good spirit.
examples.translation: They joked teasing their teacher’s quirky habits, but it was all in good spirit.

examples.sentence: If you think you can get away with that, you have a laugh coming — she’s tougher than you think.

examples.sentence: We went to the concert to have a laugh and enjoy ourselves after a stressful week.

examples.sentence: I’m planning to have a laugh this weekend with my friends at the amusement park.
examples.translation: I’m planning to have fun this weekend with my friends at the amusement park.

examples.sentence: They had a laugh all night long, dancing and singing without a care in the world.
examples.translation: They enjoyed themselves all night long, dancing and singing without a care in the world.

examples.sentence: My toddler definitely has a mind of his own; he refuses to eat anything but pizza.
examples.translation: My toddler is independently minded; he refuses to eat anything but pizza.

examples.sentence: You can’t force her decisions because she has a mind of her own and thinks for herself.
examples.translation: You can’t force her decisions because she is independently minded and thinks for herself.

examples.sentence: Even if everyone else agrees, he’ll have a mind of his own and choose differently.
examples.translation: Even if everyone else agrees, he will form his own opinions and choose differently.

examples.sentence: That old car has a mind of its own; sometimes the engine starts and sometimes it doesn’t.
examples.translation: That old car seems to behave unpredictably; sometimes the engine starts and sometimes it doesn’t.

examples.sentence: My computer has a mind of its own today — it keeps freezing without any warning.
examples.translation: My computer behaves unpredictably today — it keeps freezing without any warning.

examples.sentence: Watch out for the door; it seems to have a mind of its own and slams shut randomly.
examples.translation: Be careful with the door; it behaves unpredictably and slams shut randomly.

examples.sentence: Do you have a moment to discuss the new project timeline?

examples.sentence: I’ll have a moment after lunch to review your report thoroughly.

examples.sentence: She had a moment of panic when she realized she lost her passport at the airport.

examples.sentence: Everyone has a moment of weakness sometimes, so don’t be too hard on yourself.

examples.sentence: He had a moment during the speech and forgot what he was going to say next.

examples.sentence: With so many regulations and paperwork, starting this business feels like having a mountain to climb.

examples.sentence: They prepared all the gear they needed to have a mountain to climb safely and successfully.

examples.sentence: She has a mountain to climb next weekend, and she's been training for months.
examples.translation: She will climb an actual mountain next weekend and has been training for months.

examples.sentence: He had an accident on the highway last night but thankfully escaped without serious injuries.

examples.sentence: The factory had an accident when some machinery malfunctioned, causing a minor fire.

examples.sentence: If you have an accident at work, report it immediately to your supervisor.

examples.sentence: They called an ambulance after the cyclist had an accident and fell off his bike.

examples.sentence: She had an accident during the long car ride because there were no rest stops nearby.

examples.sentence: The artist’s painting was admired because she has an eye for color and detail.

examples.sentence: Humans have an eye for recognizing faces even in blurry images.

examples.sentence: Having an eye for subtle differences helps surgeons perform delicate operations.

examples.sentence: If you have an eye for fashion, you’ll know which styles are trending this season.

examples.sentence: She’s famous because she has an eye for architectural detail that most people miss.

examples.sentence: If you think the project will be easy, you have another thing coming once the real work starts.

examples.sentence: They believed the negotiations would be smooth, but they had another thing coming when talks broke down.

examples.sentence: Don’t assume you can bypass the rules; if you do, you’ll have another thing coming.
